themeWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God; rendered as LORD (Yahweh/Jehovah).
Righteousness, justice, rightness; conformity to God’s standard.

sentenceTheme: David offers a morning plea for the LORD’s righteous guidance and protective favor against the wicked.

verses.text: Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.

highlightedWords.meaning: Word, matter, message; spoken or written communication.
The personal name of Israel’s God; rendered as LORD (Yahweh/Jehovah).

verses.text: Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.

highlightedWords.meaning: King, ruler; one who reigns with authority.
God; the supreme God (also used of gods/judges in some contexts).

verses.text: My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.

highlightedWords.meaning: Morning, dawn; the early part of the day.
Prayer, supplication; a petition addressed to God.

verses.text: For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; the supreme God (also used of gods/judges in some contexts).
Wickedness, injustice, wrong; moral evil or guilt.

verses.text: The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.

highlightedWords.meaning: To hate, detest; to regard with aversion.
Iniquity, trouble, vanity; perversity or wickedness.

verses.text: Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.

highlightedWords.meaning: Falsehood, lie, deceit.
To abhor, detest; to regard as abominable.

verses.text: But as for me, I will come into thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.

highlightedWords.meaning: Lovingkindness, covenant loyalty, mercy.
To bow down, prostrate oneself; to worship.

verses.text: Lead me, O LORD, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.

highlightedWords.meaning: Righteousness, justice, rightness; conformity to God’s standard.
Way, road, path; manner or course of life.

verses.text: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part is very wickedness; their throat is an open sepulchre; they flatter with their tongue.

highlightedWords.meaning: Throat; the organ of speech and breathing.
Tongue; language or speech.

verses.text: Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; the supreme God (also used of gods/judges in some contexts).
Transgression, rebellion; willful breach of law.

verses.text: But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.

highlightedWords.meaning: To seek refuge, take shelter; to trust.
To cover, hedge in; to protect or defend.

verses.text: For thou, LORD, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as with a shield.

highlightedWords.meaning: To bless, endue with power for prosperity and well-being.
Large shield, buckler; protective covering in battle.
